name: Iran
summary: Day 52 Sunday. SNSC INSTITUTIONAL RATIFICATION — civilian apex body (chaired by Pezeshkian, statutorily outranks IRGC and FM) issued evening statement read on IRIB April 18 declaring Iran 'determined to enforce monitoring and control over transit through Strait of Hormuz until the definitive end of the war.' Operationalises IRGC Saturday framework: transit certificates, $1/bbl fees on loaded tankers (empty free), military and 'hostile' vessels denied passage. Araghchi's Friday 'completely open' framework is institutionally suspended. The three-way split (civilian / IRGC / Supreme Leader) the graph tracked is OPERATIONALLY COLLAPSED for the first time since March 20 Trump ultimatum — civilian-wing endorses hardline posture. ARAGHCHI REBUKE SHARPENS: Tasnim/Fars/Kayhan now individually name the FM and demand accountability; IRNA does not defend. FM still 'reviewing new US proposals' — not removed but bargaining authority visibly circumscribed. RIAL at 1.527M/USD Apr 18 Alanchand print (step-down from 1.6M+ mid-April peak); still below 2M trigger. Islamabad Round 2 reportedly Monday April 21 — still single-source (CNN), no delegation travel corroborated. India MEA summoned Amb. Fathali Saturday evening (first formal India-Iran diplomatic friction of crisis). SNSC willingness to accept further economic pain while re-closing Hormuz signals hardliner ascendancy has lower currency-pain sensitivity than civilian track.

signals.content: US (Operation Epic Fury) and Israel (Operation Lion's Roar) launched coordinated surprise airstrikes on multiple sites across Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, Chief of Staff Mousavi, and former president Ahmadinejad. Weapons: Tomahawks, B-2/B-1/B-52 bombers, HIMARS. 201 killed, 747 injured on Day 1 across 24 provinces.

Iranian state media confirmed Khamenei's death. Iran responded with 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones targeting Israel, US bases, and Gulf states hosting US assets (UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Jordan).

IRGC announced Strait of Hormuz closed to US, Israeli, and Western allied ships. Missile launch rates declining from Day 1, analysts point to depletion of missile/launcher stores and rationing strategy for longer war.
Mojtaba Khamenei (age 56, son of Ali Khamenei) appointed new Supreme Leader by Assembly of Experts. His mother, wife, and sister were killed in the Feb 28 strike. He was not present at the compound.
Iranian government reported 1,255 killed in US-Israeli attacks, described as 'mostly civilians.' Independent group Hengaw later reported 5,300 killed in first 18 days (511 civilians, 4,789 military).

Mojtaba Khamenei issued first statement (read by state TV anchor, no video/audio of him released). Called for national unity, Hormuz to remain closed, demanded all US bases in region be closed or attacked.

Iran's FM Araghchi rejected ceasefire: 'The United States started it and is responsible for all consequences.' President Pezeshkian set conditions: US/Israel must recognize Iran's rights, pay reparations, guarantee no future war.

Israel struck Iran's South Pars gas field (world's largest, 70% of Iran's gas production) and Asaluyeh refinery. Damage affected ~12% of total gas output. Iran retaliated by striking Qatar's Ras Laffan, UAE gas fields, Saudi refinery, Kuwaiti gas units.

Iran categorically denied Trump's claim of 'productive conversations.' Foreign Ministry stated no negotiations, direct or indirect, are taking place. Fars and Tasnim (both IRGC-linked) confirmed zero contact with Washington. Mehr News Agency described Trump's remarks as attempt to 'influence energy markets and gain time.' An informed Iranian source claimed Trump's pause reflects pressure, not progress.

Iran fired new missile barrage at Israel including Tel Aviv, causing major building damage and at least 4 casualties. Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf called Trump's talk claims 'fakenews used to manipulate financial and oil markets.' IRGC issued sharp rebuke calling Trump a 'deceitful American president.' However, CBS News exclusively reported a senior Iranian FM official said 'we received points from the US through mediators and they are being reviewed' — Iran publicly denies but privately engaging via intermediaries. FM Araghchi stated Hormuz is 'open to ships from nations not backing US-Israeli aggression' — a selective blockade shift designed to split the coalition. Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr appointed new SNSC secretary replacing assassinated Ali Larijani. Tasnim published Iran's 'six conditions to end the war,' seeing 'no immediate prospect for ceasefire.' Police arresting citizens 'spreading propaganda in favor of the enemy'; internet blackout in 25th day.

Iran rejected US 15-point ceasefire plan, calling it 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable.' Issued 5-point counterproposal: end aggression, guarantee no recurrence, reparations, end all fronts, sovereignty over Hormuz. FM Araghchi: 'We do not want a ceasefire. We want the war to end on our terms.' (Sources: NPR, CNBC, Al Jazeera, PBS, The Hill)

IRGC Navy chief Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri killed in Israeli airstrike at Bandar Abbas at 3:00 AM. He was personally overseeing Hormuz closure. Confirmed by IDF, CENTCOM, Netanyahu. US Admiral told IRGC Navy to 'abandon your posts or die.' Iran has NOT confirmed death. Highest-ranking kill since Supreme Leader Khamenei on Feb 28. (Sources: IDF, CENTCOM, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Jerusalem Post, Gulf News)

IRGC formalized yuan-denominated toll booth system at Hormuz. Ships submit details to IRGC intermediaries, receive code and escort through Iranian territorial waters around Larak Island. At least 2 vessels paid $2M each in yuan. 26 ships used system since March 13. 142 total transits March 1-25 vs 2,652 baseline = 95% reduction. (Sources: USNI News, Fortune, Foreign Policy, Al Jazeera, NBC, Lloyd's List Intelligence)

CONFIRMED: Iran struck Prince Sultan Air Base (Saudi Arabia) with 6 ballistic missiles and 29 drones — 15+ US troops injured, 5 seriously, some aircraft damaged. Total US wounded past 300. Iranian missiles struck Tel Aviv commercial street killing Vyacheslav Vidmant (52), injuring 11+. IRGC threatened US university campuses in Gulf with Monday noon deadline. Bushehr nuclear plant hit for third time in 10 days — IAEA warns of leak risk but no radiation detected. Tasnim (IRGC-linked) reported formal response to US 15-point proposal sent through intermediaries — contradicting public 'no negotiations' posture. CENTCOM claims 10,000+ strikes and 92% of IRGC Navy's largest vessels destroyed. Air raid sirens across Kuwait, Bahrain, Israel early Sunday March 29. (Sources: NPR, Fortune, Al Jazeera, Fox News, WaPo, Times of Israel, Tasnim, CENTCOM/C-SPAN, IAEA)

Trump claimed Sunday that Iran agreed to 'most of' the 15-point demands (Air Force One, CNN). Pakistan offered to host direct US-Iran talks. Pentagon simultaneously preparing 'weeks of limited ground operations' including Kharg Island raids — 82nd Airborne + 2 MEUs deploying. IRGC struck ALBA (Bahrain) and EGA (UAE) aluminum plants — new industrial targeting category. IRGC university deadline TODAY noon. Iran army targeted Haifa military aerospace + Ben Gurion fuel storage. (Sources: CNN, Bloomberg, NPR, Al Jazeera, CNBC, JPost)

EVENING UPDATE: (1) IRGC university deadline PASSED at noon Tehran time (~1:30 PM IST) without confirmed attack on Gulf university campuses. US did NOT issue condemnation demanded. AUIB moved to remote learning as precaution. Non-follow-through reduces IRGC credibility on future deadlines. (2) Iran struck Kuwait power and water desalination plant — Indian worker killed. Kuwait relies on desalination for ~90% of drinking water. New targeting category: civilian water infrastructure. (3) US-Israeli strikes hit Tehran power distribution infrastructure causing blackout (since restored). (4) Saudi intercepted 5 Iranian ballistic missiles targeting Eastern Province. Targeting expansion continues: military→energy→industrial→water infrastructure. (Sources: Al Jazeera liveblog, The National, India TV, GMA News)

MORNING UPDATE: Trump escalated to 'obliterate' threat via Truth Social — threatening to destroy all electric plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, 'and possibly all desalination plants' if Hormuz not 'immediately' reopened. Iran VP Aref responded: 'Trump can only decide about sending troops to Khark; but when it comes to bringing them back, the decision is no longer his. Because no one returns home from hell.' FM spokesperson Baghaei: in 31 days, 'no negotiations with America' — only 'submission of a negotiation request through intermediaries.' Ghalibaf (Speaker): 'Our missiles are in place,' accused US of 'pursuing through diplomacy what it failed to achieve in war.' Iran's Khatam Al-Anbiya blamed Israel for Kuwait desalination strike (no evidence provided — Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi attribute to Iran). IRGC-linked Tasnim warned of 'new fronts at Bab al-Mandeb' if US conducts ground operations on Iranian islands. IDF struck 170 targets 'throughout Tehran' in 24 hours including weapons production sites, causing blackout (restored). Mojtaba Khamenei Day 31 without video/audio — leaked audio claims 'minor leg injury.'

EVENING UPDATE — Multi-layered developments: (1) WSJ BOMBSHELL: Trump willing to end war without Hormuz reopening — Leavitt called it 'not a core objective.' Directly contradicts 'obliterate' post conditioning destruction on Hormuz opening. (2) 82nd Airborne confirmed in theater with division-level HQ. 31st MEU arrived. Total force ~55,000+. Pentagon preparing 'weeks of limited ground operations' including Kharg Island raids (WaPo) but White House says NO authorization. (3) US struck Isfahan overnight (early March 31) — site houses ~540 kg 60%-enriched uranium. Iran's AEOI confirmed strike, said 'no casualties or radiation leaks.' IAEA cannot verify. Bulletin of Atomic Scientists: Iran transferred entire HEU stockpile to Isfahan pre-war. (4) Iran missile fire slowed to 10-15/day vs ~90/day Day 1 — depletion or rationing. (5) Khatam al-Anbiya announced doctrine shift 'defensive to offensive' (RHETORIC — no confirmed new offensive operations; contradicted by declining missile rate). (6) Committee of Four (Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia) met in Islamabad, created formal diplomatic mechanism. Pakistan hosting 'direct US-Iran talks in coming days' — most specific commitment to date. Iran has NOT confirmed attendance. (7) Hezbollah 600+ attacks on Israel in 24 hours — doubling prior high. 4 IDF killed, 3 UN peacekeepers killed. France called emergency UNSC. (8) Iran's 5 counter-conditions (reparations, sovereignty over Hormuz, end sanctions, Lebanon included) — each individually a dealbreaker. April 6 deadline 6 days out — probability of third extension HIGH.

Iran's President Pezeshkian told EU Council President António Costa that Iran has 'the necessary will' to end the war — the most conciliatory language from Iran's civilian government since Day 1. Iran's 5-point counterproposal to US 15-point plan includes: reparations, sovereignty over Hormuz, end all sanctions, Lebanon included — each individually a dealbreaker. FM spokesperson Baghaei maintains 'no negotiations with America.' The public-denial/private-engagement pattern continues: Tasnim reported Iran sent formal response to US 15-point proposal 'through intermediaries' on March 29. (Sources: Press TV, IRNA, Tasnim, Bloomberg)

IRGC issued explicit threat to strike facilities of 18 named US companies: Apple, Microsoft, Google, Meta, IBM, HP, Intel, Tesla, Boeing, Nvidia, JPMorgan, and others. Deadline: 20:00 Tehran time April 1. IRGC claiming companies play 'direct role in planning and tracking targets.' Evacuation warnings issued to employees within 1km. Represents NEW CATEGORY of escalation: targeting private-sector corporate infrastructure. Previous IRGC deadline (university campuses, March 30 noon) expired without action, reducing IRGC deadline credibility. IRGC conventional missile capacity declined to 10-15/day vs 90/day on Day 1. (Sources: Fars News, Tasnim, Times of Israel, The Hill, Tom's Hardware)

Iranian drone struck Kuwait-flagged VLCC Al-Salmi at Dubai port anchorage zone, 31 nautical miles NW of Dubai (~12:10 AM local time March 31). Carrying ~2M barrels oil (1.2M Saudi, 800K Kuwait), bound for Qingdao, China. Hull damaged, fire erupted and extinguished. No oil spill. All 24 crew safe. Most significant single-vessel attack of the war — hitting fully laden VLCC at UAE port anchorage. Concurrent attacks: Greek-owned container ship off Ras Tanura, Thai cargo ship Mayuree Naree near Hormuz (3 crew missing). Iran's parliament drafting toll legislation to formalize Hormuz authority — potentially making selective control PERMANENT. (Sources: Bloomberg, Gulf News, Al Jazeera, The National, Maritime Executive)

Day 33 without video or audio of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. CIA/DIA testimony of 'deep command and control crisis' remains uncontradicted. Iran has provided only written statements read by state TV anchors. CONFIRMED ABSENCE continuing. (Sources: previous CIA/DIA testimony, Iran International)

IRGC corporate deadline (April 1, 20:00 Tehran) resulted in single strike — missiles hit Batelco telecom facility in Hamala, Bahrain hosting AWS infrastructure. Pattern established: maximalist threats, minimalist execution. University deadline (Mar 30) produced no attack. Corporate deadline (Apr 1) produced one AWS facility strike. S&P 500 rallied +2.9% despite the threat — market discounting IRGC deadline credibility. (Sources: Free Press Journal, Gizmodo, Daily Sabah, Bahrain Interior Ministry)

RHETORIC: President Pezeshkian published open letter to American people on X/Twitter on April 1: 'The world stands at a crossroads. Continuing along the path of confrontation is more costly and futile than ever.' Released hours before Trump's primetime address — deliberate counter-programming. CREDIBILITY CAVEAT: Per The Week India, Pezeshkian is in 'complete political deadlock' — IRGC blocks presidential appointments, controls information flow between Pezeshkian and Supreme Leader via military council. (Sources: Al Jazeera, The Hill, Press TV, The Week India)

Iran's missile fire rate collapsed from 480 ballistic missile launches Day 1 to <25/day by early April — 95% decline. Pre-war arsenal ~2,500 ballistic; ~2,410 fired in first 10 days. 75% of launch capacity destroyed per CENTCOM. HOWEVER, Iran adapted: pivot to firing 1-2 missiles at civilian/commercial infrastructure (tankers, airports, data centers, desalination plants), and RFE/RL reports hit rate has been INCREASING. Quality over quantity. (Sources: CENTCOM, Jerusalem Post, RFE/RL, CSIS, JINSA)

Three-way internal divergence at HIGH intelligence value — civilian government (Pezeshkian peace signals, open letter) vs IRGC (corporate threats, selective strikes, escalation) vs reformist media (calling for 'pragmatism' and 'national cohesion'). When these consolidate, position locks. Current divergence = internal decision NOT yet made. April 6 deadline may force resolution. (Sources: Al Jazeera, The Week India, IRNA vs Tasnim/Fars)

Iran's FM Araghchi stated prepared for 'at least six months' of war — negotiation-positioning (simultaneously lists specific END conditions: guarantees, compensation, sovereignty). Rubio-Araghchi meeting did NOT materialize. Iran maintains 'no direct talks held with US, contacts limited to messages via mediators.' (Sources: Anadolu Agency, Al Jazeera, Time)

Iran struck Qatar tanker AQUA 1 near Ras Laffan with 3 cruise missiles (2 intercepted, 1 hit), 17nm north of Ras Laffan. IRGC claimed responsibility. Iran reportedly gave Qatar advance notice. Second vessel attack in 2 days (after Al-Salmi VLCC at Dubai). Attack pattern expanding: Hormuz to Dubai to Qatar waters. (Sources: QatarEnergy statement, Al Jazeera, AGBI)

US-Israeli strikes hit steel plants (Mobarakeh, Khouzestan), pharmaceutical facilities, Qeshm Island desalination plant, Bandar Abbas passenger pier on April 1-2. Targeting expanded from military to energy to industrial to civilian infrastructure. 2,000+ Iranian deaths per official figures; Hengaw independent report suggests 5,300+. (Sources: Al Jazeera, CBS, NPR)

Hormuz toll law passed FULL Majlis (not just committee). Codifies Tehran's sovereignty/control over Strait, establishes transit fees payable in Iranian rials, bans US/Israel-linked vessels. Needs Guardian Council review + presidential signature. De facto ~$2M/vessel. GCC condemned — 'will not accept bilateral arrangements.' (Sources: Turkiye Today, Anadolu, CNN, Bloomberg)

TARGETING EXPANDING TO PHARMACEUTICALS: US-Israeli forces bombed Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical raw material units in Tehran — R&D department destroyed, 'blow to national medical supply chain' per Iranian official. Shahid Haqqani passenger pier in Bandar Abbas bombed. Multiple cities hit: Ahvaz, Shiraz, Isfahan, Karaj, Kermanshah, Bandar Abbas. Steel plants, pharma, port infrastructure targeted in same wave. Targeting categories now: military→energy→industrial→water→university→corporate→pharmaceutical. FM Baghaei: Trump ceasefire claim 'false and baseless.' Pezeshkian demands confidence-building measures (strikes pause) before direct talks — circular dependency with US position. Missile depletion continues (~2,410 of 2,500 expended) but accuracy improving per RFE/RL. (Sources: Al Jazeera liveblog, Iranian state media, RFE/RL, CENTCOM, The Hill)

F-15E STRIKE EAGLE SHOT DOWN OVER IRAN — first US fighter jet downed in the war. Both crew rescued by April 5 after dramatic CIA-assisted extraction operation. Weapons system officer evaded capture 24+ hours hiding in mountain crevice. Iran's IRGC claimed they also downed 2 C-130s and 2 Black Hawks during rescue. WSJ reports MC-130Js got stuck and were deliberately destroyed by US forces — Iran may be claiming credit for US self-destruction of stuck aircraft. IRNA misidentified wreckage as 'F-35.' Iran called on public to hand over American soldier, attempted to capture pilot before extraction. Highest-profile US air loss of the conflict.

Iran categorically rejected Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum (issued ~April 3). Ebrahim Azizi (parliament National Security Committee chair): 'Iran will send you and your soldiers straight to the depths of hell.' General Abdollahi Aliabadi (Khatam al-Anbiya): called Trump 'helpless, nervous, unbalanced, and reckless.' FM Baghaei continued denying negotiations. April 6 deadline now hours away with zero diplomatic movement.

US-Israeli strikes hit Mahsahahr Petrochemical Zone (Khuzestan Province) — 5 killed, 170+ wounded. Largest single-site industrial casualty event of the war. Targeting ladder now expanded: military→energy→industrial→water→university→corporate→pharmaceutical→petrochemical. 30+ universities targeted since war start.

Iran struck Kuwait Petroleum Corp HQ (Shuwaikh complex, houses oil ministry) with drones — building evacuated, set ablaze. Also struck Bahrain's Bapco Energies oil storage tank. UAE petrochemical plant fires. Iran now targeting energy infrastructure across FOUR Gulf states simultaneously (Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE, Qatar). Represents major geographic escalation of Iran's retaliatory strikes against Gulf hosting nations.

Iran media tone shifted to TRIUMPHALIST after F-15 shootdown — IRGC stream now dominant over Pezeshkian peace signals in state media coverage. Three-way internal split persists but IRGC winning the narrative war. Pezeshkian peace signals still exist but increasingly marginalized. US casualties updated: 13 KIA, 365 WIA (up from 348). Iran official casualties: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ wounded.

CONFIRMED: Deadline EXTENDED 20 hours to Tuesday April 7, 8 PM ET. Iran's response: categorical rejection. Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf: 'Make no mistake: You won't gain anything through war crimes.' NSC chair Azizi: Iran will send adversaries 'to the depths of hell.' FM Araghchi dismisses Trump's ultimatum, condemns threats as 'incitement to war crimes.' IRGC counter-threat: 'If you hit electricity, we hit electricity.' IRGC threatened PERMANENT Hormuz closure: 'The Strait of Hormuz will be completely closed and will not be opened until our destroyed power plants are rebuilt.'

IRGC CODIFIED MIRROR-ESCALATION DOCTRINE (CONFIRMED rhetoric): 'If you hit electricity, we hit electricity.' Ghalibaf extended: 'Immediately after power plants and infrastructure in our country are targeted, vital infrastructure as well as energy and oil infrastructure across the entire region will be considered legitimate targets and will be irreversibly destroyed.' IRGC urged Gulf civilians to 'urgently leave locations where American forces are stationed so that no harm comes to you' — a pre-strike warning mirroring US 'knock warnings.' IRGC appeared to walk back earlier threats against desalination plants (water infrastructure), though broader energy infrastructure remains targeted. Both sides escalating on the same axis.

REPORTED (Axios): 45-day ceasefire framework under discussion. Mediators: Pakistan, Egypt, Turkey + direct Witkoff-Araghchi texts. Iran's sticking points: Hormuz reopening and HEU stockpile — won't give up either for only 45 days. Mediator assessment: 'slim' chances. Iran rejected 15-point US plan as 'unreasonable,' submitted counter-conditions: war damage compensation, guarantees against future conflict, full war end across all fronts including resistance groups, sovereignty over Hormuz. Araghchi confirmed direct talks with Witkoff but denied they constitute 'negotiations.'

Haifa cluster bomb warhead strike (April 5) — 2 dead recovered, 2 still trapped under rubble. IDF assessed cluster bomb warhead — significant escalation in warhead type. ~170 ballistic missiles launched in April 4-5 window. Separately struck Tel Aviv — civilian woman killed, 27 injured. BAZAN refinery in Haifa also hit. Israeli total war casualties: 6,833 evacuated to hospitals.

Iran media tone shifted to MAXIMALLY DEFIANT — codifying mirror-escalation doctrine. The three-way split (civilian/IRGC/reformist) appears to have COLLAPSED INTO UNIFIED DEFIANCE: even Araghchi (most diplomatic figure) calling Trump's threats 'incitement to war crimes.' If negotiating posture (reject publicly, accept privately), it's the most aggressive version. If genuine, Tuesday is catastrophic.

Israel struck South Pars petrochemical plant (Iran's largest, half of petrochemical output) and Mahshahr petrochemical complex (shutting all production by destroying Fajr 1/2 utility plants). Israel also struck three Tehran airports overnight (Bahram, Mehrabad, Azmayesh), destroying dozens of helicopters/aircraft. IRGC responded with 'New Persian Gulf Order' declaration — Hormuz will 'never return to its former state.' After US-Israeli strike on Sharif University AI/GPU data center, IRGC designated 18 Western tech companies (Apple, Microsoft, Google, Nvidia, Tesla, Oracle, JPMorgan, Boeing, Meta) as 'legitimate targets' and threatened UAE's $500B Stargate AI project. Brigadier General Majid Khademi (IRGC Intelligence Chief, effectively No. 2 of IRGC intelligence) killed in precision Israeli strike — most significant decapitation of IRGC intelligence capability since war began, surpassing Tangsiri assassination. Quds Force commander Asghar Bagheri also eliminated. Iran rejected 45-day 'Islamabad Accord' ceasefire, submitted 10-clause counter-proposal demanding permanent war end, sanctions relief, reconstruction reparations, and Hormuz protocol. Trump's Tuesday 8 PM ET deadline is TODAY. Fars News reported 6 children under 10 killed in Tehran residential airstrike (REPORTED — not independently confirmed by Western outlets). Cumulative: 3,540+ killed (1,616 civilians including 244 children). (Sources: Euronews, i24News, CNBC, Times of Israel, Xinhua, Fox News, Axios, Al Jazeera, Fars News)

ISLAMABAD ACCORD COLLAPSED — IRAN REJECTS, ISSUES 10-POINT COUNTER [CONFIRMED]. Iran transmitted formal 10-point counter via Pakistan: (1) permanent end to war (no temporary truce); (2) end to all 'acts of aggression' by US/Israel; (3) guarantees war will not recur; (4) compensation for war damages and reparations; (5) reconstruction of damaged infrastructure; (6) end of war 'across all fronts' including resistance groups; (7) recognition of Iranian sovereignty over Strait of Hormuz; (8) 'safe-passage protocol' for Hormuz traffic (Iranian regulation, not free reopening); (9) lifting of sanctions; (10) no deadlines accepted while Iran reviews proposals. Trump: 'significant but not good enough.' This is the maximalist Iranian position — sovereignty over Hormuz + reparations + sanctions lifting + war crimes accountability = US/Israel acceptance of defeat. Drop Site cited a senior Iranian official directly. Times of Israel ran the formal Iranian foreign ministry statement.

IRGC INTEL CHIEF MAJ. GEN. MAJID KHADEMI KILLED in Israeli decapitation strike (same operation as South Pars #2). Asghar Bakeri, leader of the IRGC Quds Force undercover unit, ALSO killed. Most senior IRGC intelligence loss of the war. Iranian state media confirmed Khademi killed (rare admission). Israel claims responsibility on the record (Defense Minister Katz). Eliminating IRGC's intelligence chief during ceasefire negotiations is itself a signal: Israel does not believe a deal will hold. Iran's domestic propaganda has notably NOT acknowledged Khademi's death (despite Iranian state media confirming it) — gap between performative diplomacy and actual losses.

SOUTH PARS PETROCHEMICAL HIT FOR SECOND TIME [CONFIRMED]. Israel struck the petrochemical plant inside the South Pars natural gas field — world's largest gas field, shared with Qatar. Defense Minister Katz: 'a powerful strike on the largest petrochemical facility in Iran' — plant responsible for HALF of Iran's petrochemical output. Combined with last week's strike on Iran's #2 petrochemical site, ~85% of Iran's petrochemical production is now offline. Wider strikes April 6: Israel + US carried out a wave of attacks killing more than 25 people in Iran. Most significant Israeli strike of war by economic impact + decapitation.

FIRST FORMALLY COORDINATED TRIPARTITE CRUISE MISSILE/DRONE SALVO [CONFIRMED]. Iran + Hezbollah + Houthis launched a barrage of cruise missiles and drones against Israel April 6 — first formally announced tripartite operation of the war. Houthi statement: the three allies 'launched a barrage of cruise missiles and drones targeting several vital and military sites belonging to the Israeli enemy.' Axis-of-resistance escalation milestone. CRITICAL: Houthi participation here targets ISRAEL ONLY — Red Sea shipping STILL untouched on Day 39. Sophisticated escalation/restraint bifurcation.

Maintained maximalist nuclear position (relayed via Trump-Netanyahu call): refusing US demand for full HEU stockpile surrender + permanent prohibition on resuming enrichment. Pre-war declared stockpile: 440.9 kg of 60% U-235 (per IAEA, no access for 8+ months). Iran's #1 bargaining chip is precisely this stockpile. To accept the US demand is to accept destruction of strategic deterrent. There is no plausible Iranian 'yes' to this condition — even if a 45-day ceasefire is signed, the nuclear demand will be the structural blocker that collapses it.

Drone strike on Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait — 15 US troops wounded overnight April 6-7. First major US casualty event since F-15 shootdown. Iran absorbing the cost of striking a US base hosting air operations against Iran the night before Trump's deadline. CONFIRMED ACTION — but does NOT activate the 'Iran-strikes-US-base' threshold trigger; this is incremental (drone-on-base attacks have happened before). Activation count increment.

EVENING UPDATE: IDF executed 'wide-scale wave of strikes targeting dozens of infrastructure sites' hours before Trump's 8 PM ET deadline — three Tehran airports struck (aircraft/helicopters targeted), Shiraz Marvdasht petrochemical complex hit (nitric acid for ballistic missiles), railway warning issued to all Iran until 9 PM, multiple bridges struck near Kashan/Qom. Netanyahu claimed 70% of Iran's steel production destroyed. Combined with prior week, ~85% of Iran's petrochemical production offline. Over 150 aircraft involved in operations. IRGC Intelligence Organization formally vowed 'Operation Crushing Revenge' for Khademi killing — same naming convention as Soleimani retaliation 2020. No IRGC intelligence chief successor named — significant command gap (2nd consecutive chief killed). Separately, Times of Israel reported (single diplomatic source, adversarial intel basis) Mojtaba Khamenei 'unconscious and receiving treatment in Qom in severe condition, unable to govern.' ISW/Critical Threats assessed 'military council of senior IRGC officers has assumed core decision-making power.' Pezeshkian claimed 14M Iranians volunteered to fight. Human chains called around power plants — dual framing: civilian shields + propaganda advantage. Iran's formal 10-point counter transmitted via Pakistan — 7 of 10 points now public including permanent end to hostilities, security guarantees, sanctions lift, Hormuz 'safe passage protocol' ($2M transit fee). (Sources: ABC News, Al Jazeera, PBS, NBC News, Times of Israel, ISW/Critical Threats, i24NEWS, Military.com)

IRAN SNSC FORMALLY ACCEPTS 2-WEEK CEASEFIRE — CLAIMS 'HISTORIC VICTORY' [CONFIRMED]. Iran's Supreme National Security Council issued a formal statement accepting the 2-week ceasefire. Key points: (a) Iran 'achieved a historic victory by forcing the criminal US to accept its 10-point plan,' (b) the SNSC explicitly invoked 'guidance of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei' — first official assertion of his functional authority since Day 1, (c) FM Araghchi specified the deal includes 'safe passage' through the Strait 'via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces,' (d) 'this does not signify the termination of the war — our hands remain upon the trigger,' (e) Iran's Supreme Leader instructed all military units to cease fire approximately two hours after Trump's announcement, (f) talks open Friday April 10 in Islamabad. The semantic gap between Trump's 'COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING' and Iran's 'controlled passage via coordination' is the structural fault line. Iran's claim that the US 'accepted' the 10-point plan is one-sided — Trump called it a 'workable basis to negotiate.'

IRGC STRIKE ON SADARA $20B ARAMCO-DOW JV AT JUBAIL [CONFIRMED]. The IRGC formally claimed responsibility for striking the Jubail industrial hub explicitly 'in response to the enemy's crimes in the aggression against Iran's Asaluyeh petrochemical plants.' Used medium-range missiles + multiple suicide drones. Targets: Sadara Chemical (the $20 billion Saudi Aramco-Dow Chemical JV), additional SABIC plants, ExxonMobil-linked facilities. Saudi MoD intercepted seven ballistic missiles with debris near energy facilities. Sadara filed Tadawul disclosure that rated capacity has gone to ZERO overnight: 1.5 Mt/y ethylene, 750 Kt PE, 330 Kt propylene oxide, 400 Kt MDI, all offline with no restart timeline. SABIC's Jubail operations represent ~11% of Saudi non-oil GDP and 6-8% of global petrochemical output. Most significant single piece of Saudi industrial damage of the war and is BAKED IN regardless of the diplomatic outcome.

IRAN CLUSTER MUNITIONS HIT ~50 SITES IN CENTRAL ISRAEL [CONFIRMED]. Iranian ballistic missile cluster munitions struck approximately 50 locations across central Israel as part of the '99th wave of Operation True Promise IV' under codename 'Ya Fatima al-Zahra (PBUH).' At least 6 wounded including a seriously wounded woman in Petah Tikva and a moderately wounded man in Ramat Gan. Cluster munitions are designed to defeat Iron Dome interception by saturating dispersal areas. With Iran's Supreme Leader cease-fire order issued ~2 hours after Trump's announcement, this is likely the LAST major Iranian salvo of the current phase.

14-day Pakistan-brokered ceasefire fractured within 18 hours. Israel launched 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — 50 IAF jets struck 100 Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, killing 254. Iran RE-CLOSED Hormuz citing Israeli strikes as ceasefire violation. IRGC issued 'regret-inducing response' threat via Telegram/Sepah News. IRGC Aerospace Force issued 'emergency, exceptional instructions for unprecedented retaliation against Israel' per MEMRI monitoring. Three simultaneous crises on Ceasefire Day 1.

IRNA-Tasnim alignment BROKEN within 18 hours — IRGC diverging from civilian government on ceasefire. IRNA (state/civilian) pivoted from 'historic victory' to violation framing that PRESERVES the deal. Tasnim (IRGC-linked) pivoted to violation framing that BUILDS THE CASE TO EXIT — citing anonymous 'senior security source' saying Tehran 'assessing the possibility of exiting the deal.' Press TV escalated to 'Iran has become a new superpower' while running betrayal framing. The divergence PATTERN is intelligence about internal power structure — civilian government vs IRGC split.

Iran International reported President Pezeshkian confronted IRGC commander Hossein Taeb on April 4 in a 'tense and highly charged exchange,' accusing IRGC commanders of 'acting unilaterally and driving escalation through attacks on regional countries.' Pezeshkian warned Iran's economy 'would not be able to withstand a prolonged war.' IRGC reportedly rejected Pezeshkian's intelligence minister candidates and forced its own appointment — Guards described as having 'effectively stripped the government of executive control.' Single source: Iran International (diaspora/opposition, Saudi-funded, but has credible IRGC sourcing).

Lavan Island oil refinery attacked after ceasefire took effect (~10 AM local April 8). Iranian state television confirmed the attack. UAE Mirage 2000-9 jets suspected per Turkiye Today and OSINT analysts; Abu Dhabi has NOT confirmed or denied. Iran launched retaliatory strikes against UAE and Kuwait within approximately one hour.

Iran launched massive missile and drone attacks on multiple Gulf states within hours of ceasefire taking effect: UAE (17 ballistic missiles + 35 drones intercepted), Saudi Arabia (5 BMs + 9 drones, East-West Pipeline pumping station struck), Bahrain (6 missiles + 31 drones), Qatar (7 missiles + drones), Kuwait (28 drones). Abu Dhabi Habshan gas complex caught fire. Assessment split: Solace Global attributes to 'breakdown of command and control'; alternative reading: pre-ceasefire launches still in flight or IRGC units operating independently.

Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf accused the US of violating THREE clauses of the 10-point ceasefire plan: (1) continued Israeli attacks on Lebanon, (2) drone in Iranian airspace, (3) enrichment demands. Pakistan PM Sharif contradicted both Trump and Netanyahu, stating ceasefire covers 'everywhere including Lebanon' — creating a three-way contradiction on ceasefire scope.

Day 43. IRNA-TASNIM SPLIT WIDENING. IRNA (civilian): diplomatic language, 'ball is in US court' (Araghchi). Tasnim (IRGC): 'Israeli Violation to Result in Exit from Deal,' 'Restraint is Over,' threatening to 'obliterate US-linked infrastructure.' Pezeshkian-IRGC confrontation corroborated by Ynet (originally Iran International): accused IRGC commanders Vahidi/Abdollahi of 'unilateral escalation.' IRGC reportedly blocked Pezeshkian's intelligence minister appointment. Iran released 10-point Islamabad proposal: continued Hormuz control, enrichment rights, sanctions lifting, no-aggression commitment. Saudi-Iranian FM held FIRST call since war (The National). Delegations confirmed: Ghalibaf (lead) + Araghchi for Saturday Islamabad formal talks. White House says Iran agreed to 'turn over enriched uranium' — Iran denies. (Source: Tasnim, IRNA, Iran International, Ynet, Time, The National, Al Jazeera)

Day 44. ISLAMABAD TALKS BEGINNING — proximity format. Both delegations arrived: Iran's Ghalibaf (lead) + FM Araghchi + SNSC Secretary Ahmadian + Central Bank Governor Hemmati + several MPs at Serena Hotel, Islamabad. Arrived late Thursday night at Nur Khan Airbase. Pakistani fighter jets escorted Iranian delegation plane. Format: PROXIMITY TALKS — separate rooms, Pakistan shuttling messages, NOT face-to-face. Ghalibaf set TWO preconditions before negotiations begin: (1) ceasefire in Lebanon, (2) release of Iran's blocked assets. Ghalibaf on X: 'These two matters must be fulfilled before negotiations begin.' Rhetoric hardline-conditional: 'We have goodwill, but we do not have trust.' Cited three alleged ceasefire violations: continued Israeli strikes on Lebanon, drone entering Iranian airspace, denial of enrichment rights. Trump told reporters Iran has 'no cards,' flatly rejected enrichment: 'There will be no enrichment of Uranium.' IRGC POWER GRAB DEEPENING (REPORTED by Iran International/NewsX, NOT confirmed): Ahmad Vahidi blocked all Pezeshkian intelligence minister nominees including Dehghan, stated 'all critical and sensitive leadership positions must be selected and managed directly by the IRGC until further notice.' Pezeshkian in 'complete political deadlock' — accused Vahidi and Abdollahi of 'acting unilaterally and driving escalation through attacks on regional countries,' warned those policies 'had destroyed any remaining chance of a ceasefire' and were steering Iran toward 'a huge catastrophe.' Pezeshkian warned economy faces 'total collapse within three weeks to one month' without ceasefire. Security cordon around Mojtaba preventing government access — Pezeshkian's meeting requests 'all gone unanswered' (REPORTED, Iran International). CRITICAL QUESTION FOR ISLAMABAD: does the Iranian delegation actually have authority to commit Iran, or will any agreement be subject to IRGC veto? IRGC mine map published — permanent Hormuz control mechanism. Oman formally rejected toll proposal citing UNCLOS.

Day 45. Islamabad talks FAILED after 21 hours across April 11-12 with no deal. Format evolved: initial proximity talks (separate rooms, Pakistan shuttling) began ~2 PM local Saturday, then shifted to trilateral face-to-face talks including Iranian officials from ~5 PM — first time US and Iranian principals sat in the same room since 1979. Iran's ~70-member delegation led by Ghalibaf and Araghchi faced Vance's ~300-member US delegation. Core impasse: US demanded 'an affirmative commitment that Iran will not seek a nuclear weapon, not just now, not just two years from now, but for the long term.' Iran refused this formulation, which goes BEYOND JCPOA framework. Vance left with 'final and best offer.' Iran's spokesperson Baqaei said success depends on 'acceptance of Iran's legitimate rights and interests.' CRITICAL DIAGNOSTIC: IRNA and Tasnim CONVERGED on identical 'US overreach' blame framing — NOT the IRNA-Tasnim split from April 9. Tasnim: 'due to US overreach and ambitions, the two sides have so far failed to reach agreement.' This convergence means IRGC and civilian government are ALIGNED on post-Islamabad narrative, neither faction building internal case against the other. Failure framed as external (US fault) not internal (factional dispute) — PRESERVES possibility of a second round. Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar called the session 'intense and constructive' and urged both sides to maintain the ceasefire. Two-week ceasefire (April 8-22) remains technically in effect.

POST-TALKS INSTITUTIONAL ALIGNMENT STRENGTHENED (afternoon update): Three distinct institutional signals confirm unified Iranian positioning. (1) Iran government X post: "Negotiations will continue despite some remaining differences" -- most constructive signal, explicitly preserving diplomatic track. (2) FM spokesperson Baghaei: "It is natural that we shouldn't have expected to reach agreement in just one session" -- normalizing the failure, not catastrophizing it. (3) Iran stated "in no hurry to resume negotiations" unless US agrees to "reasonable" deal -- maintaining Hormuz leverage. Fars News (IRGC-linked) ran dismissive but NOT destructive narrative: "Americans needed talks only to restore lost international image." CRITICALLY: No IRGC military commander has broken unified narrative for 18+ hours post-talks. The institutional alignment is STRONGER than morning assessed -- no crack between civilian government and IRGC over the talks. Assessment: sophisticated post-failure messaging -- blame US externally, preserve unity internally, keep diplomatic door open. (Sources: Iran government X post, FM Baghaei via Al Jazeera/NPR/CBS, Free Press Journal, Fars News via News9Live)

IRGC broke 30+ hours of post-Islamabad silence. IRGC Navy stated Hormuz remains under Iran's 'full control,' open for non-military vessels, military ships get 'forceful response.' Ghalibaf addressed Trump: 'If you fight, we will fight.' No formal response to 'final offer' — deliberate ambiguity. IRNA/Tasnim/Fars unified on 'US overreach' framing. FM Baghaei: 'should not have expected agreement in one session.' Tasnim: 'Ball in US court, Iran in no hurry.' No SNSC emergency session. IRGC called US blockade a ceasefire violation. (Sources: Al Jazeera, NPR, Tasnim, IRNA, Press TV, JPost)

Iran called US blockade 'piracy' and a 'grave violation of sovereignty' — IRGC, Iran Army, and UN Ambassador Iravani all condemned. IRGC warned 'no port in the Gulf will be safe' if Iranian ports are threatened and declared 'maximum combat readiness.' However, NO confirmed IRGC kinetic action on blockade Day 1 — zero hostile engagements, no mining operations, no attacks on US vessels. The bark-without-bite pattern continues from the university and corporate deadline episodes. (Sources: CBS News, Al Bawaba, Xinhua, Report.az, PressTV — 5+ independent sources)

Iran's response to blockade differentiated into five simultaneous tracks rather than a single unified message: (1) DIPLOMATIC — Pezeshkian told Macron Iran will negotiate 'within framework of international law,' keeping the European channel alive. (2) MILITARY-DECLARATORY — IRGC Khatam al-Anbiya warned 'no port safe,' vowed 'new forms of warfare.' (3) NUCLEAR — Iran rejected US demand for 20-year enrichment moratorium, counter-offered with 'single-digit' years (reported as 5); offered to DILUTE HEU stockpile rather than transfer it out of country. (4) REGIONAL ESCALATION — demanded reparations from five Arab states; Iraqi militias continue drone attacks on Gulf states despite ceasefire. (5) DOMESTIC — Pezeshkian: 'national resistance shattered enemy dream of collapse.' The multi-vocal response may reflect absence of Mojtaba Khamenei's unified direction or deliberate multi-track strategy. (Sources: ISW, PressTV, Middle East Eye, Xinhua, CBS News, Anadolu Agency — 6+ independent sources)

Trump claimed 'Iran reached out' and 'wants to deal very badly,' stating 'the right people' called the White House. Iran has NOT confirmed any outreach. AP reported (via Middle East Eye, NDTV) possible Thursday talks via Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt facilitation with two venues under consideration — but this comes from mediators and unnamed officials, not confirmed by either principal. Vance separately stated 'the next step in negotiations is up to Iran. US has all the cards.' Trump's CLAIMED contact triggered massive market moves (oil -1.4%, S&P +1.0%) but remains unverified. (Sources: Bloomberg, CNBC, AP via Middle East Eye, NDTV — 4+ independent sources)

ISW reports Iran rejected US demand for 20-year enrichment moratorium but counter-offered with a 'single-digit' number of years (reported as 5). Iran also offered to DILUTE its highly enriched uranium stockpile rather than TRANSFER it out of country — a significant concession signal not publicly known before. Six unresolved nuclear issues: enrichment cessation, facility dismantlement, HEU stockpile removal, regional security, ending terrorist group funding, Hormuz reopening. Iran holds 440.9 kg of 60%-enriched uranium (enough for ~10 weapons if further enriched). IAEA access terminated Feb 28. (Sources: ISW, Anadolu Agency, IranWire/WSJ — reported by multiple sources but not confirmed by principals)

Reuters reported (April 11) that Mojtaba Khamenei has 'severe and disfiguring wounds' including facial and leg injuries, and may have 'lost a leg.' He is conscious but incapacitated — decision-making may be operating through proxies (Velayati, IRGC leadership). Day 47+ without video/audio appearance. Ali Akbar Velayati (Supreme Leader's adviser) stated April 12 that 'the key to the Strait of Hormuz is firmly in Iran's hands' — channeled through Velayati rather than Mojtaba directly. The multi-vocal Iranian response may reflect absence of a single authoritative voice. (Source: Reuters — single source for wound severity, REPORTED)

Robin Brooks (Brookings Institution, former IIF chief economist) assessed that the blockade will trigger hyperinflation in Iran: 'As Iran's oil exports collapse, there'll be no cash for imports, so activity implodes, the currency goes into a devaluation spiral, and hyperinflation ensues.' Tehran prices already up ~40% since war onset; rial down 8% on black markets. Iran losing approximately $435M/day ($13B/month) in revenue from the blockade. (Source: Robin Brooks/Brookings analysis — analyst assessment)

Iran demanded reparations from five Arab states (reported as UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar) for their role in facilitating US-Israeli strikes on Iran. This is an escalatory diplomatic action directed at regional neighbors designed to drive wedges between the US and its Arab partners and position Iran for post-war leverage. Gulf states have not publicly responded. (Sources: Middle East Eye, The National — 2 independent sources, REPORTED)

Day 48. FM Araghchi posted on X: 'In intensive talks at highest level in 47 years, Iran engaged with U.S in good faith to end war. But when just inches away from Islamabad MoU, we encountered maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade. Zero lessons earned. Good will begets good will. Enmity begets enmity.' — the most specific public framing of Iran's negotiating narrative. Critically: Araghchi's 'inches away from Islamabad MoU' is BLAME-FRAMING, NOT door-closing — it preserves the narrative that a deal was within reach and the US walked away, which is exactly the framing you use to justify returning to talks. Iran has NOT publicly rejected a second round. A mediating country diplomat told reporters that 'Tehran and Washington had agreed to more talks' (REPORTED — unnamed single source). MP Kowsari stated Iran will continue talks 'to expose US behavior' — hostile framing but door explicitly open. AP citing 5 sources reported delegations keeping Friday-Sunday (April 17-19) open for second round, likely in Islamabad — this is the LAST window before ceasefire expires April 21. Iran's government damage assessment: $300B-$1T total war damage (per Haass/Britannica compilation). Losing ~$435M/day ($13B/month) in oil revenue from blockade (per Robin Brooks/Brookings). Tehran consumer prices up ~40% since war onset, rial down 8% on black markets. Hyperinflation dynamics emerging — which creates increasing economic incentive for Iran to return to the table regardless of public posture. IRGC via Tasnim: 'all traffic under full control of armed forces,' 'any wrong move will trap the enemy in deadly whirlpools' — rhetoric maximal, action minimal (ZERO kinetic response Day 2-3 of blockade). (Sources: Araghchi X post, AP/Reuters, Richard Haass Substack, Britannica, BusinessToday, Robin Brooks/Brookings, IRNA, Tasnim)

CONFIRMED: Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir led delegation (Interior Minister Naqvi, FM officials, security/technical experts) to Tehran April 15 to deliver US message to FM Araghchi and prepare framework for second round of talks. Araghchi received delegation; publicly thanked Pakistan for hosting first round and stated Iran's 'commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region remains firm' — the most conciliatory FM-level Iranian language since the April 8 SNSC ceasefire acceptance. This is the first CONFIRMED bilateral contact between Washington's proxy (Pakistan) and Tehran since Islamabad collapsed April 12, and directly contradicts Vance's April 12 'final and best offer' framing. Shuttle format is the classic post-collapse revival pattern. White House confirmed April 15 via press secretary Leavitt that the next round of US-Iran talks is 'very likely' to be held at the same Islamabad venue; AP sources (via Middle East Eye) suggest delegations still holding April 17-20 window. Corroborated by Press TV, Bloomberg, Al-Monitor, PBS, Tribune, Geo TV, Middle East Monitor. (Sources: Press TV, Bloomberg, Al-Monitor, PBS, Tribune, Geo TV, Middle East Monitor)

CONFIRMED: UAE Deputy PM Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan held phone call with Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf on April 15 — FIRST high-level UAE-Iran contact since the war began Feb 28, and specifically since Iran's April 8 salvo (17 ballistic missiles + 35 drones) on Abu Dhabi's Habshan gas complex which prompted UAE to recall its ambassador and close its Tehran embassy. Focus: 'de-escalation' (WAM framing). Iran has publicly demanded reparations from the UAE (among five Gulf states); the call suggests both sides are parking that demand to stabilize immediate tensions. Gulf-Iran backchannel running parallel to Pakistan-mediated US-Iran track — classic multi-track pattern during final stretch before ceasefire renewal decision. Saudi Arabia has NOT made a similar call publicly; MBS quieter than Abu Dhabi on every de-escalation signal of the past week. Both sides confirm call occurred; framings are consistent, suggesting readout was negotiated. (Sources: WAM, Press TV, WANA, Al-Monitor, Rudaw, Xinhua, Tribune India/ANI)

CONFIRMED ACTION: Iran central bank issued 10-million-rial banknote — largest single denomination in Iranian monetary history — just one month after introducing 5-million-rial note in March. Central bank internal assessment reportedly concluded economy may take 12 YEARS to rebuild after war. Unofficial rial trading near 1.6 million per dollar (vs ~1.0M pre-war, -60%). Consumer inflation near 50%; Fortune reports prices up 40% since war began six weeks ago; Iran authorities privately worry about meeting government payroll. Together these constitute CONFIRMED hyperinflation trajectory — validates Robin Brooks/Brookings blockade-triggers-hyperinflation thesis by Iran's OWN monetary policy response. The 10-million-rial note is the unambiguous tell that Iran's economy is inside the hyperinflation corridor, which compresses the decision window to weeks not months. Hyperinflation closes the exit ramp from talks — either Iran makes a deal on diminishing terms OR doubles down on asymmetric escalation (Houthis, Hezbollah, direct strikes on US forces) to change the bargaining frame. (Sources: Iran International, Fortune, Robin Brooks/Brookings)

Day 4 US blockade: CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper doubled down — 10 vessels turned around, 'ZERO ships have broken through.' Military resources deployed: 12+ warships (USS Abraham Lincoln, USS Tripoli, multiple DDGs), 100+ aircraft, 10,000+ personnel. Legal Insurrection/HotAir (Republican-aligned) amplify 'fully implemented, halting 90% of Iran's sea trade.' CNN counter-framing: 'So why are some ships going through Strait of Hormuz?' — noting the Strait transit vs. Iranian port distinction. Yesterday's evening brief documented Al Jazeera/Kpler confirmation of 3+ sanctioned vessels transiting Day 1 (M/T Elpis, M/T Rich Starry). CENTCOM's Day 4 'ZERO' claim either covers only post-Day 1 activity, counts only ships actually heading to Iranian ports, or overstates. Iran's counter-blockade (announced April 13, all Gulf/Sea of Oman ports) remains DECLARATORY — Day 4 with ZERO confirmed IRGC kinetic action against vessels bound for UAE/Saudi/Kuwait/Qatar ports. Continues maximal-rhetoric/minimal-execution pattern (RHETORIC — IRGC restraint either preserves ceasefire corridor OR reflects degraded operational capacity post-Khademi April 7 + 10,000+ US-Israeli strikes). (Sources: CENTCOM, Al Jazeera, CNN, NBC News live blog, Military Times, FDD, Washington Post)

State media coordination on de-escalation: IRNA and Tasnim BOTH ran Araghchi's post-Munir statement as the lead item — institutional alignment stronger than at any point since Islamabad talks concluded. Typically IRNA soft/Tasnim aggressive (civilian-IRGC split); today's convergence preceded the April 6-8 ceasefire acceptance and is now recurring. Press TV framed Munir visit as neutral mediator-framing ('Pakistan's chief of army staff arrives to deliver US message') rather than 'Pakistan cowed by US.' Fars News (IRGC-linked, usually most aggressive) has NOT issued new Hormuz kinetic threats in 48 hours. Assessment: directed or permitted by whoever is making decisions in Mojtaba's incapacity (military council per ISW, Velayati per April 12 signals). (Sources: IRNA, Tasnim, Press TV, Fars News)

IRAN RE-LINKS LEBANON TO IRAN-US TRACK [CONFIRMED OFFICIAL STATEMENT, RHETORIC for framing]. Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei stated April 17 that the 10-day Lebanon ceasefire is 'part of a broader agreement with the US to pause the regional war, mediated by Pakistan' and credited Islamabad's diplomatic push. He reiterated Iran's demand that Israel 'fully withdraw forces from southern Lebanon' — directly contesting Netanyahu's 10km security zone. Separately, Baghaei attacked US sanctions threats as 'economic terrorism and state-sponsored extortion… crimes against humanity and… genocide.' The re-linkage matters: (1) Iran views Islamabad precondition scorecard as partially met, creating framework for claiming progress; (2) puts 10km zone directly on Round 2 table, which Washington would prefer to compartmentalize; (3) the genocide/terrorism framing sits alongside conciliatory ceasefire welcome — the standard two-track signaling, not a break from it. Ghalibaf parallel 'approaching with caution' statement (via Al Jazeera/CNN) is consistent with this two-track pattern — softer than his April 11-12 'inches from MoU' rhetoric. (Sources: Baghaei via Press TV/BSS/Al Jazeera, Tribune India/ANI on Ghalibaf, CNN April 17 liveblog)

TASNIM 'TRILATERAL TALKS' CLAIM — UNCONFIRMED [CLAIMED]. Iran's semi-official Tasnim News Agency reported 'delegations had begun a new round of trilateral talks' while noting 'significant challenges remained.' No US State Department or Pakistani MFA confirmation of any new trilateral session. Treat as Iranian information operation until corroborated. Separately, Tasnim reported UAE Vice President Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan phoned Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf to discuss 'regional developments and ways to reduce tensions' — CONFIRMED UAE-Iran parliamentary channel contact. The UAE track parallels Saudi's: both Gulf states maintaining daily Iran contact while publicly urging de-escalation to Washington. Two-track messaging tightening, not loosening: IRNA/MFA conciliatory + Press TV 'genocide' framing + Tasnim 'trilateral talks' optimism running in coordinated parallel. (Sources: Tasnim via Al Jazeera aggregated coverage, Al Jazeera April 17 liveblog)

ARAGHCHI HORMUZ-OPEN DECLARATION + TASNIM REBUKE [CONFIRMED]. FM Abbas Araghchi posted on X on April 17 (~17:00 EST): 'In line with the ceasefire in Lebanon, the passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz is declared completely open for the remaining period of ceasefire.' This produced the largest single-day oil-price move of the crisis — Brent -7.6% to $91.87 (wire-ICE $90.38, -9.1%), WTI -11.3% to $84.00, Heating Oil -12.8% to $3.34 — and S&P 500 record close 7,126.06 (+1.2%). Tasnim News Agency (IRGC-linked) published a rebuke within hours calling the post 'a flawed and incomplete tweet' that 'created unnecessary ambiguity about reopening the Strait of Hormuz' — the sharpest public IRGC-civilian divergence since the April 9 ceasefire breakdown. Operational reality is qualified: Iran's Ports and Maritime Organisation pre-designated a 'coordinated route' and passage remains via IRGC permission only. Kpler showed 8 commercial vessels transited by 2pm EST (vs ~120/day pre-war, ~10-15% of normal); actual oil-tanker traffic still near-zero. The declaration is thus fenced by IRGC operational control and tied to the Lebanon pause. (Sources: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, NBC News, Argus Media, Kpler/MarineTraffic via DNYuz, Tasnim News Agency)

BAGHAEI 'RECIPROCAL MEASURES' CONDITIONAL [CONFIRMED STATEMENT, RHETORIC/THREAT for operational content]. Iran FM spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei clarified Araghchi's 'completely open' Hormuz statement: 'If the maritime blockade of Iran's ports continues, Iran will take reciprocal measures.' This is the first explicit public conditional: Hormuz opening is hostage to US port-blockade posture. If Monday Islamabad round is delayed or fails AND US maintains port blockade, Iran has publicly committed to 'reciprocal measures' — potentially re-closure of Hormuz transit, IRGC fast-boat harassment, or symmetric interdiction of US-linked vessel. Elevates binary risk over Monday-Tuesday window. No reciprocal action taken yet; Hormuz 'open' declaration still in effect. US (Trump, CENTCOM) no direct response; Trump reiterated 'blockade in full force' until deal — structurally opposed positions remain in place. (Sources: Tribune India, ANI, Webindia123, Iran MFA primary)

IRGC NAVY FORMAL PERMISSION REGIME [CONFIRMED OPERATIONAL POLICY]. On April 17-18, Iran's IRGC Navy 'detailed conditions for vessels seeking to transit through the Strait of Hormuz, stating that all ships would require its permission to pass, with civilian vessels allowed only through designated Iranian routes, while military vessels remain barred from transit.' This is the first formal codification of the permission regime the IRGC has been operating informally since the blockade began. Combined with Araghchi's 'open' claim and Baghaei's reciprocal-measures warning, Iran's position is now structured as: Hormuz is 'open' by Iranian grace, only through Iranian-designated lanes, only for civilian vessels, only while Lebanon ceasefire holds AND US port blockade lifts. This IS the 'controlled passage via coordination' paradigm from the April 8 ceasefire text, now formalised operationally. (Sources: Tribune India/ANI, Webindia123, Bloomberg via claims journal)

HARDLINER-CIVILIAN FAULT LINE RE-OPENS [CONFIRMED institutional divergence]. Three data points converge on a fresh IRGC-civilian government tension: (1) Tasnim publicly rebuked sitting FM Araghchi's Hormuz-open tweet — rare on-record rebuke of Iranian FM by major state-affiliated outlet; (2) Kayhan editor Hossein Shariatmadari had published 'A Ceasefire Is a Multi-Layered Gift to the Enemy' hours before April 8 ceasefire, arguing Washington cannot be trusted, then reversed to hail the ceasefire as 'victory' post-announcement — telegraphed hardline positioning pattern; (3) IRGC-affiliated Fars News denied any substantive negotiations with Trump in initial stages. Meta-signal: hardline faction is preserving capacity to delegitimise whatever Araghchi brings back from Monday's Islamabad round. April 16 convergence (IRNA+Tasnim aligned) is broken by April 17-18 on the Hormuz framing. Karim Sadjadpour (Carnegie): falsifiable test — if Tasnim rebuke softens within 72 hours, IRGC is falling in line; if it sharpens with new attacks on 'sell-out' diplomats, factional split is real. (Sources: Tasnim, Kayhan newspaper, Fars News, Al Jazeera liveblog)

SNSC FORMAL RATIFICATION OF IRGC HORMUZ CLOSURE [CONFIRMED — 4 independent sources]. Iran's Supreme National Security Council — civilian apex body chaired by President Pezeshkian that by statute outranks both IRGC and Foreign Ministry — issued evening statement (read on state broadcaster IRIB late Saturday IST, carried as lead item on Tasnim, Press TV, WANA, Al Jazeera) declaring Iran is 'determined to enforce monitoring and control over transit through Strait of Hormuz until the definitive end of the war and the establishment of lasting peace.' Statement operationalises IRGC Saturday framework: (1) full vessel information collection, (2) 'transit certificates' per Iran's wartime regulations, (3) fees of $1/bbl on loaded tankers (empty free) for 'security, safety, and environmental protection services.' Military and 'hostile' (US-flagged, Israeli-linked) vessels explicitly denied passage. SNSC frames US blockade as 'ceasefire violation' and declares Araghchi's Friday 'conditional and limited reopening' is SUSPENDED until US lifts blockade. This is the first time since March 20 Trump ultimatum that Iran's civilian apex has formally institutionalised the IRGC position — three-way factional split (civilian / IRGC / Supreme Leader) is operationally collapsed. Confirms Sadjadpour Apr 18 falsifiable test resolved toward 'hardliner ascendancy' not 'factional split.' (Sources: Press TV, Tasnim, Washington Examiner, Al Jazeera live-blog)

ARAGHCHI REBUKE SHARPENS TO INDIVIDUAL-NAMING [CONFIRMED institutional signal]. Per Iran International (diaspora, Saudi-funded, reliable on regime dissent), state media issued 'rare and sharply worded criticism of Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and the negotiating team' after his Friday X post. Fars News (semi-official, IRGC-aligned): 'Iranian society was plunged into a haze of confusion.' Tasnim: post lacked 'any verbal explanation or at least sufficient written clarification' — 'complete poor judgment in communication.' Kayhan (editor Hossein Shariatmadari appointed by late Ali Khamenei) joined Fars in demanding Araghchi answer for the declaration. Al Jazeera independently corroborates. In Iran's state-media architecture, this is institutional signalling, not journalism — Shariatmadari and Tasnim both naming a sitting FM and demanding accountability is a political shot across the bow of Pezeshkian's civilian track, and functions as cover for IRGC/SNSC operational override. IRNA (civilian-line state wire) did not carry or amplify criticism — accommodation rather than participation. Araghchi has not resigned, not been replaced, and per Al Jazeera is 'currently reviewing' new US proposals — FM has not been institutionally removed from the track, but its bargaining authority is visibly circumscribed. Sadjadpour's 72-hour falsifiable test (Apr 18) has resolved toward factional sharpening, not softening. (Sources: Iran International, Al Jazeera, NCRI — hostile source, corroborative only on Kayhan angle)

IRANIAN RIAL 1.527M/USD SATURDAY PRINT [REPORTED — thin-volume channel]. Alanchand.com published April 18 IRR/USD remittance rate at 1,527,000; bonbast-reported free-market rates have oscillated between 1.42M (December 2025 record) and a mid-April peak above 1.6M. Still below 2,000,000/USD hyperinflation-spiral threshold (Brooks-Brookings thesis) that anchors the 'Iranian rial breaks 2M' trigger (added April 16). 12-month move −3,046% (Toman basis); annualised inflation ~42%; CBI governor Farzin resigned December 29, 2025. Read-across: SNSC's willingness to re-close Hormuz while accepting further economic pain suggests either (a) regime assesses pain is already priced in and marginal deterioration buys little leverage for Washington, or (b) hardliner ascendancy (IRGC/SNSC/Mojtaba axis) has lower sensitivity to currency pain than the Pezeshkian-Araghchi civilian track. Monday print will test whether SNSC statement + persistent blockade produces a further step to 1.7M+. Trigger held at watching — one step closer; moves stepwise not linear. (Sources: Alanchand.com primary, bonbast secondary, Trading Economics charts)

INDIA MEA SUMMONS IRANIAN AMBASSADOR [CONFIRMED — first formal India-Iran friction of crisis]. India's Ministry of External Affairs summoned Iran's Ambassador to India, Dr Mohammad Fathali, for meeting with Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri Saturday evening IST. Misri conveyed 'deep concern at the shooting incident involving two Indian-flagged ships in the Strait of Hormuz' — specifically naming Sanmar Herald (VLCC, ~2M bbl Iraqi crude, bridge-window damage) and Jag Arnav (forced westward retreat) — and urged Iran to 'resume at the earliest the process of facilitating India-bound ships.' Misri reminded Fathali that Iran had earlier facilitated safe passage of India-bound ships (reference to pre-April 18 Chabahar-framework de-confliction). Fathali 'undertook to convey these views to Tehran.' India maintained strategic-autonomy silence through March bombing, Khamenei elevation, US blockade, and every prior Hormuz escalation — this is the first formal Indian protest of the entire 52-day crisis. Framing deliberately narrow — 'deep concern' (not condemnation), urging resume facilitation (not stop harassment) — preserves India's lane to restart, but diplomatic ratchet is unprecedented. Tehran-side MFA no public response. (Sources: The Wire, Tribune India, NewsX, The Federal)

edges.context: Day 52. SNSC FORMAL RATIFICATION April 18 — civilian apex body institutionalises IRGC permission regime. Transit certificates + $1/bbl fees on loaded tankers (empty free). Military and 'hostile' vessels denied. Araghchi's Friday 'completely open' framework SUSPENDED until US lifts port blockade. Civilian-IRGC-Supreme-Leader three-way split operationally collapsed. Kpler: ~2 oil/gas tanker transits since April 8 ceasefire (~1-2% of pre-war ~120/day). Edge transformed from conditional-reopening to codified state control; Iran is operationalising a priced chokepoint as a strategic instrument. Paper-physical gap widened to ~$40/bbl at Friday close.

Day 49. Hezbollah launched 105 attack waves on Israel April 15 — most intense single day of 2026 war (Alma Research). Lebanon death toll 182. Ghalibaf's Islamabad preconditions included Lebanon ceasefire; Hezbollah escalation is direct pressure instrument on US-Iran track. Iran-Hezbollah linkage explicit. Netanyahu 'ready to renew strikes on Iran'; IDF chief Zamir approved further plans — RHETORIC, no execution.

Day 52. SNSC statement frames US blockade as 'ceasefire violation' and suspends Araghchi's Friday framework until US lifts it — structurally opposed positions hardened. Round 2 Islamabad reportedly Monday April 21 — still SINGLE-SOURCE (CNN, Iranian sourcing); no Witkoff/Vance/Kushner travel corroborated; Pakistani MFA silent; Iranian delegation travel not confirmed. Araghchi per Al Jazeera 'reviewing new US proposals' — paper lane alive but no travel. CENTCOM has NOT publicly corroborated or responded to Saturday kinetic ~16-18 hours later; Pentagon silent on specifics. Bessent April 18: Iranian oil general license 'will not be renewed' (waiver T-0 = Monday 05:30 IST). Trump: 'productive conversations' vs 'drop bombs again' — two-track rhetoric maintains small off-ramp. The White House has not briefed any outlet first — administration-deliberate silence is itself intelligence.

Day 49. Hezbollah 105 attack waves April 15 = most intense day of war. Lebanon 182 dead single-day (highest of war). Hezbollah political council: 'We are not bound by what they agree to' — attacking on Iran track regardless of Rubio's April 14-15 Israel-Lebanon talks. Confirms Iran-proxy escalation during ceasefire-expiry week is pressure-instrument on US-Iran Round 2, not end-of-war de-escalation.

REVERSAL from retaliatory_strikes mode. CONFIRMED ACTION April 15: UAE Deputy PM Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed called Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf — FIRST high-level UAE-Iran contact since Feb 28 war began. Focus explicitly on 'de-escalation' (WAM). Iran's reparations demand parked. Multi-track pattern: Gulf-Iran backchannel running parallel to Pakistan-mediated US-Iran track. UAE moving first; Saudi Arabia NOT making similar call publicly.

Iran struck Ras Laffan LNG (Trains 4 & 6 destroyed). Qatar expelled Iranian attaches. AQUA 1 tanker struck 17nm north of Ras Laffan (IRGC gave advance notice). Qatar now running mediator role (Al Jazeera surfaced UAE-Iran call first — standard Qatari pattern to see diplomatic warming first). QatarEnergy downstream urea still HALTED per Carnegie/Brownfield — 3-5yr LNG repair timeline intact.

Iran struck Kuwait power/water desalination plant (Mar 30, Indian worker killed). Iran struck Kuwait Petroleum Corp HQ (Shuwaikh complex, houses oil ministry) with drones April 4-5 — building evacuated, set ablaze. Kuwait relies on desalination for ~90% of drinking water. Two major infrastructure hits in one week.

IRGC struck Batelco telecom facility (Hamala, hosting AWS infrastructure) April 1-2. Iran struck Bahrain's Bapco Energies oil storage tank April 5. Bahrain hosting US Fifth Fleet — primary retaliatory target. Energy infrastructure now directly targeted.

Day 52. SNSC formal re-closure + $1/bbl fee instrument converts Hormuz into a priced chokepoint. Friday settles ($91.87 ICE / $90.38 wire) now look like paper-market theatre on Araghchi's since-suspended framework. Paper-physical gap (~$40/bbl) widened to crisis-widest with Dated Brent ~$132 physical vs $91 futures. Monday Globex open (Sunday 22:00 GMT) = first re-pricing venue. Eurasia Group's Brew: 'Iran now holds effective control over the critical oil chokepoint' — few if any ships through while dueling blockades remain; US retail gasoline $5/gal by summer thesis. Morgan Stanley $110 Q2 call validated by SNSC institutional codification. Goldman $90 base case / $115 severe case: Saturday + Sunday intel = precisely the severe-case tail. Iran oil-revenue loss ~$435M/day unchanged; rial 1.527M Apr 18 print.

Houthis are Iran's proxy in Yemen. Now announced entering war on Iran's side. Threatening Red Sea strikes. Would create second chokepoint. Tasnim warned of 'new fronts at Bab al-Mandeb' if US conducts ground ops.
Russia providing intelligence to Iran including data on US military positions.
China provided missile spare parts, advanced radar systems, BeiDou-3 encrypted navigation, satellite intelligence.

IRGC corporate deadline resulted in single AWS strike at Batelco Bahrain — maximalist threats, minimalist execution. S&P 500 rallied +2.9% despite IRGC threat to 18 named US companies. Market discounting IRGC deadline credibility. Pattern: university deadline (no attack), corporate deadline (one facility).

trigger_points.condition: Mojtaba Khamenei appears on video/audio (proof of life and command)
trigger_points.mechanism: New leader hasn't been seen. CIA/DIA testified Iran in 'deep command and control crisis.' If he appears, signals regime stability. If not, signals possible internal fracture. Day 49 on 2026-04-16 — still no video/audio. Reuters: severe wounds, possible leg loss. Decision-making via proxies (Velayati, military council).

trigger_points.condition: Iranian rial breaks 2,000,000/USD black market (Brooks hyperinflation thesis confirmation)
trigger_points.mechanism: Brooks/Brookings: blockade triggers 'devaluation spiral and hyperinflation.' Current rial ~1.6M/USD black market (vs 1.0M pre-war). Iran central bank's 10M rial note issuance + 12-yr rebuild estimate confirms direction. If rial breaks 2M, hyperinflation closes exit ramp — Iran either accepts materially worse deal OR doubles down on asymmetric escalation. Testable 30-day window. If rial stabilizes at 1.2M or strengthens without a deal, thesis is wrong.

trigger_points.condition: Iran's missile stockpile depleted to point of inability to sustain retaliation
trigger_points.mechanism: Missile launch rates already declining. If Iran can't sustain retaliation, Hormuz enforcement weakens.

trigger_points.condition: Iran accepts direct talks with US (publicly acknowledged)
trigger_points.mechanism: Would be biggest de-escalation signal. Currently receiving intermediary messages while publicly denying. Public acknowledgment would crash oil, rally equities.

trigger_points.condition: Iran retaliatory strike for Tangsiri assassination
trigger_points.mechanism: Historical pattern: Iran retaliated for Soleimani killing (2020 Ain al-Asad strike). IRGC Navy chief assassination at Bandar Abbas may trigger escalated naval action at Hormuz or strikes on Israeli/US assets. No retaliation confirmed yet as of March 28.

trigger_points.condition: Iranian internal consolidation — civilian SNSC, IRGC, and Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei all aligned on the ceasefire acceptance with no visible factional dissent. Mojtaba's name explicitly invoked as authorising the decision. ACTIVE.
trigger_points.mechanism: As of April 6, the three-way split APPEARS to have collapsed into unified defiance — even Araghchi (most diplomatic figure) calling threats 'incitement to war crimes.' Ghalibaf codified mirror-escalation. IRGC issued civilian evacuation warning. | UPDATE 2026-04-07: ISLAMABAD ACCORD REJECTION + 10-POINT MAXIMALIST COUNTER is now the strongest evidence of unified defiance posture. | UPDATE 2026-04-09: CONSOLIDATION REVERSED — IRNA-Tasnim alignment BROKEN within 18 hours of ceasefire. | UPDATE 2026-04-12: Post-Islamabad institutional alignment STRENGTHENED — IRNA/Tasnim/Fars converged on 'US overreach' blame framing. | UPDATE 2026-04-14: Five-track response to blockade may signal DIFFERENTIATION again (Pezeshkian European channel, IRGC military rhetoric, nuclear team 5-year counter). | UPDATE 2026-04-16: CONVERGENCE RE-OBSERVED on DE-ESCALATION AXIS. | UPDATE 2026-04-18: CONVERGENCE BROKEN AGAIN — Tasnim publicly rebuked Araghchi's Hormuz-open tweet as 'flawed and incomplete.' | UPDATE 2026-04-19: SADJADPOUR FALSIFIABLE TEST RESOLVED TOWARD HARDLINER ASCENDANCY. SNSC (civilian apex) formally ratified IRGC Hormuz-closure posture — three-way factional split is operationally COLLAPSED into unified hardline posture. Kayhan / Fars / Tasnim sharpened rebuke by individually NAMING Araghchi and demanding accountability. IRNA did not defend. Araghchi not resigned, not replaced — but bargaining authority visibly circumscribed. Pezeshkian has not publicly defended his FM. Consolidation character is now HARDLINE, not DE-ESCALATION (reverse of April 16). Monitor: Pezeshkian-Araghchi public defence within 48 hours would break consolidation; silence = durable hardliner control.

trigger_points.condition: Iran executes 'reciprocal measures' — re-closure of Hormuz transit, IRGC fast-boat harassment, or symmetric interdiction of US-linked vessel
trigger_points.mechanism: April 18 Saturday: IRGC fast-boat fire on VLCC Jag Arnav + Sanmar Herald (UKMTO, UK MoD); container ship hit by 'unknown projectile'; cruise liner Mein Schiff 4 VHF-threatened. April 19: SNSC formally ratified IRGC closure + $1/bbl fee regime. Recommendation from Senior Intel was to upgrade to active; Graph Engineer held at WATCHING (conservative): CENTCOM has NOT publicly corroborated Saturday's kinetic 16-18 hours later (Apache imagery pre-dates kinetic; no specific response). 2+ independent operational sources bar cleared by breadth (UKMTO / UK MoD / multiple named vessels / SNSC text) but NOT strictly by the original 'operational sources include US-side corroboration' reading. The March 24 Houthi error taught: rhetoric → action cascade must be resolved only on confirmed-CENTCOM bilateral operational record. If CENTCOM issues specific statement before Monday brief, OR a second kinetic event occurs in next 48 hours, trigger moves to ACTIVE. Falsifiable: if Brent gaps up 3%+ at Monday Globex open AND second kinetic within 72 hours, trigger has fired.

trigger_points.condition: Iran Supreme National Security Council formally endorses IRGC Hormuz-closure posture over civilian Foreign Ministry
trigger_points.mechanism: ACTIVATED April 19: SNSC evening statement (read on IRIB) declared Iran will 'enforce monitoring and control over transit through Strait of Hormuz until definitive end of the war.' Codifies $1/bbl transit fees and suspends Araghchi's 'completely open' framework until US lifts blockade. Four independent sources (Press TV, Tasnim, Washington Examiner, Al Jazeera). This is the first civilian-apex endorsement of IRGC operational posture since March 20 Trump ultimatum — three-way factional split operationally collapsed. Monitoring question is now reverse: does Pezeshkian publicly defend Araghchi within 48 hours? If not, consolidation is durable; if yes, factional split re-opens.

price_snapshots.trigger: SNSC formal acceptance of Pakistan-brokered 14-day pause
price_snapshots.delta_context: Iran framing: historic victory, US accepted 10-point plan, controlled passage via coordination

price_snapshots.price: Hormuz 'completely open' (Araghchi) — conditional on ceasefire + blockade lift
price_snapshots.trigger: FM Araghchi X-post during US afternoon session triggered largest single-day oil crash of crisis (Brent -7.6% to $91.87)
price_snapshots.delta_context: First Iranian formal passage-declaration since blockade began. Fenced by IRGC Navy permission regime (civilian only, designated routes). Tasnim publicly rebuked the announcement — IRGC-civilian fault line re-opens.

price_snapshots.price: SNSC formal ratification: Hormuz under Iranian transit-certificate control + $1/bbl fees; Araghchi framework suspended
price_snapshots.trigger: Iran's civilian apex body institutionalises IRGC posture — three-way factional split operationally collapsed
price_snapshots.delta_context: First civilian-apex endorsement of hardline Hormuz posture since March 20 Trump ultimatum. Converts rhetorical tool into priced instrument. Iran claims revenue while claiming sovereignty. Russia (TASS) in full endorsement of 'sovereign waters' framing; China factual; both would veto US-led UN resolution. Paper-physical gap ~$40/bbl at Friday close = crisis-widest.
